design_philosophy.core_loop: Gather resources → Advance through ages → Research technologies → Build units → Compete militarily or achieve objectives
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource scarcity and finite terrain resources (trees don't regrow) create strategic pressure; players must balance expansion, defense, and technological advancement
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Lead an ancient civilization from Stone Age hunter-gatherers to an Iron Age empire, experiencing historical progression through four distinct technological eras
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: The game enforces resource finitude as core mechanic—depleted resources cannot regenerate, making expansion and resource management existentially important rather than optional. Civilization asymmetry (each civ has unique techs/units) forces distinct strategic identities. No single 'best' civilization creates faction-based gameplay philosophy.

difficulty.checkpointing: Campaign missions structured linearly with specific objectives; victory/defeat determined by objective completion
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Random map mode with difficulty variations (e.g., 'death match' resource-heavy variant); custom scenarios allow player-created difficulty; single-player campaigns have fixed difficulty per mission

progression.upgrade_system: Four-age technological advancement system (Stone → Tool → Bronze → Iron). Technology researched at specific buildings (temples, storage pits, etc.). Military upgrades (arms/armor), economic upgrades (resource efficiency), religious upgrades (priest conversion), infrastructure upgrades (fortification strength)
progression.unlock_mechanism: Advancement to next age researched at Town Center; unlocks new units, buildings, technologies specific to that age. Civilization asymmetry: each of 12 civilizations possesses unique tech subsets—no civilization has all available technologies
replayability_hooks: 12 distinct civilizations with asymmetric unit/tech rosters
Random map generation with multiple variants (standard, death match)
Scenario Builder tool enabling user-created campaigns and custom scenarios
Multiplayer support (up to 8 players) with emergent strategic variation
Campaign scenarios for 4 civilizations (Egyptian, Greek, Babylonian, Yamato) plus Hittite demo campaign

world_building_techniques: Historical civilization selection (12 ancient cultures with authentic architectural styles)
Campaign scenarios tied to specific civilizations' histories (Egyptian, Greek, Babylonian, Yamato)
Architectural differentiation: 4 distinct architectural styles based on region (East Asian, Mesopotamian, Egyptian, Greek)
Objective-based mission narratives (e.g., Champa scenario: Khmer Empire constructs Angkor Wat while defending against invasion)
Age system mirrors historical progression (Mesolithic/Nomad → Neolithic/Chalcolithic → Bronze Age → Iron Age)

faction_indicators: 12 asymmetric civilizations with distinct architectural identities and tech rosters
Civilization choice signals strategic allegiance (each civ has unique units/techs, no single dominant strategy)
Architectural styles create visual faction differentiation: East Asian, Mesopotamian, Egyptian, Greek
danger_signals: Resource depletion mechanics create territorial competition—depleted resource zones signal contested or conquered areas
Age advancement visibility signals opponent tech level and military readiness
Military unit composition reveals civilization choice and strategic specialization
environmental_storytelling: Depleted terrain (cut forests don't regrow) shows resource exploitation history
Architecture style identifies civilization control of territory
Age-based building appearance reflects technological progression
Custom scenario objectives embed narrative into map design (e.g., landmark construction as victory condition)
